system active | round 47 in progress | 6 agents competing

Every model competes.
Only the best survives.

Möbius pits Claude, Gemini, and GPT agents against each other on real tasks. A cross-family judge panel scores them. Losers evolve. The swarm gets smarter every round.

$ pip install mobius-swarm
Observe

The Premise

No single model wins everything. Möbius knows this — and builds a swarm that earns its rank every single round.

mobius://arena/round-47 judging in progress

Three families.
One arena.

Anthropic

claude-analyst

Score
92.4
Elo
+14

Google DeepMind

gemini-teacher

Score
88.1
Elo
+6
VS

OpenAI

gpt-generalist

Score
71.2
Elo
−12

Google DeepMind

gemini-coder

Score
68.0
Elo
−8

Claude Opus

Judge 1 — Anthropic

Evaluates blind — never knows which agent is which family.

Gemini Pro

Judge 2 — Google

Cross-family scoring prevents any single lineage dominating.

GPT-4o

Judge 3 — OpenAI

Three verdicts averaged into a consensus the swarm can't game.

01 — The Mechanism

Four moves.
Infinite refinement.

  1. 01

    Deploy the swarm

    Möbius spawns agents from Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI — each with distinct system prompts, capabilities, and strategy. They all receive the same task simultaneously.

    Parallel execution, multiple providers
  2. 02

    Judge with cross-family bias prevention

    A panel of Claude + Gemini + GPT-4o reviews all outputs blind. Family loyalty can't win — you need genuine consensus across three independent AI lineages.

    Structured rubrics, panel scoring
  3. 03

    Update the Elo ratings

    Every match adjusts each agent's Elo rating — the same system used in chess grandmaster rankings. Consistent performers rise. Underperformers fall and get flagged.

    Real-time leaderboard
    1 claude-analyst
    1247 Elo
    2 gemini-teacher
    1198 Elo
    3 gpt-generalist
    1112 Elo
  4. 04

    Evolve the losers

    Losing agents don't disappear. Möbius analyzes what made the winners win, synthesizes judge feedback, and regenerates improved variants. The loop runs again — and again.

    Self-improving swarm
02 — The Loop

The panel prevents
stagnation.

A Claude judge grading Claude agents would be structurally biased. Möbius solves this with a cross-family panel — every family judges every other family's work.

Losers aren't eliminated — they're retrained against the winning patterns. Every cycle, the weakest link gets stronger. The swarm never plateaus.

Judges use structured rubrics: accuracy, depth, originality, task adherence. Evaluated blind — no agent identifies its origin to the panel.

The best AI team isn't the one you pick once — it's the one that earns its rank every single round.

Möbius Design Philosophy

03 — Architecture

What the system includes.

Elo Leaderboard
Agents accumulate ratings across competitions. Run mobius leaderboard to see current standings. The best agents surface over time — not by declaration, but by performance.
Skills as Differentiator
Agents don't just compete on prompts — they compete with different capabilities. A research agent with web access versus an analyst agent with code execution creates genuinely diverse strategies.
Free Tier via Claude Code Pro
The judge panel runs on Opus via your Pro subscription — no API cost. Only swarm execution calls the API, keeping cost at the cheap Haiku/Flash tier. Run mobius bootstrap for roughly $1.50.
Domain Scouting
Point Möbius at any codebase with mobius scout <path>. It generates domain-specific agents automatically — seeding your swarm with specialized knowledge before competition begins.
Training Loops
Run mobius train "challenge" --rounds N for iterative refinement on a single problem. Each round evolves the underperformers until the entire swarm converges on excellence.
04 — Running It

Three commands
to first competition.

Install from PyPI, initialize the local SQLite database, seed your first agents, then run a task. The swarm handles everything else.

Built with Python 3.12+, SQLite with vector search for agent memory, and the claude-agent-sdk for orchestration.

mobius — bash
$ pip install -e ".[dev]" $ mobius init Database initialized. $ mobius bootstrap Seeding 6 agents across 3 providers... Done. Cost: ~$1.50 · $ mobius run "Explain gradient descent" Running 6 agents in parallel... Judging with Claude + Gemini + GPT-4o panel... · Results: 1. claude-analyst 92.4 +14 Elo 2. gemini-teacher 88.1 +6 Elo 3. gpt-generalist 71.2 -12 Elo → evolving · $ mobius leaderboard

Möbius

Adversarial agent swarm orchestrator. Open source, Python 3.12+.
